name: csvkit (CSV Command-Line Tools)

performance.notes: Python-based; slower than Miller or xsv for very large files (> 1GB)

agent_readiness.notes: Suite of CSV manipulation command-line tools. in2csv: convert Excel/JSON/DBF/geojson to CSV. csvcut: select columns. csvgrep: filter rows. csvjoin: join CSVs. csvsort: sort. csvstat: descriptive statistics. csvsql: run SQL on CSVs (uses SQLite). csvformat: convert delimiters. csvlook: render as Markdown table. Unix pipe-friendly. Data journalism standard tool. Works seamlessly with sqlite-utils and datasette pipeline.

gotchas: Python overhead makes it slow for very large files (> 1GB) — use xsv or miller
csvsql can be slow for complex queries (better to use sqlite-utils and query directly)
No streaming SQL for extremely large datasets
best_when: Quick CSV manipulation and analysis at command line
Data journalism workflows with messy data
Converting Excel/JSON to CSV for pipeline input
Running SQL queries on CSV files without a database
avoid_when: Very large files (> 1GB) — use xsv (Rust) or miller (C)
Complex ETL pipelines (use pandas or dask)
summary: csvkit is the gold standard Unix CLI toolkit for CSV data manipulation. Convert Excel/JSON/DBF to CSV (in2csv), slice columns (csvcut), filter rows (csvgrep), join files (csvjoin), run SQL (csvsql), and generate stats (csvstat). Pipe-friendly, data journalism standard. Simon Willison's sqlite-utils and datasette integrate naturally. Slow for very large files (> 1GB) — use xsv or miller then.
